title: Phaca quinqueflora
intro: Phaca quinqueflora, also known as five-leaved knapweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to Asia, belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). The health benefits of Phaca quinqueflora include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, prevent cardiovascular diseases, and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable medicinal herb. The bioactive constituents of Phaca quinqueflora include isoflavones, saponins, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Phaca quinqueflora is often made into herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and tinctures to treat various health conditions, including cardiovascular and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Phaca quinqueflora is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the legume family, Fabaceae. It is a type of lupin that is native to the mountainous regions of southern Europe. This plant is classified as a perennial herb with a unique five-petaled flower. Within the Fabaceae family, Phaca quinqueflora is part of the subfamily Faboideae. The classification of Phaca quinqueflora is also further divided into the tribe Loteae.
